database_info.name: PTZ Camera Selection Theory - Standards Based
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for PTZ camera selection

1_ip_vs_analog_ptz.principle: IP PTZ for network systems, Analog PTZ for Turbo HD DVR systems

comparison.advantages: Higher resolution, AcuSense AI, auto-tracking, remote config
comparison.best_for: New IP installations, AI features needed

comparison.advantages: Long cable runs, works with existing coax, simpler
comparison.best_for: Existing analog systems, long distance, budget

2_dome_size_selection.principle: Dome size determines zoom power, IR range, and environmental durability

sizes.best_for: Indoor, retail, small area monitoring, discrete
Standard outdoor, parking lots, building perimeter
Large perimeter, highways, industrial, long range
Wide area + detail simultaneously, campuses, critical infrastructure

3_zoom_selection.principle: Optical zoom determines detail capture distance

zoom_guide.detail_at_50m: Fine detail, license plate at distance
zoom_guide.best_for: Large area, highway, perimeter, critical sites

3_zoom_selection.rule_of_thumb: For face identification: maximum distance ≈ zoom_factor × 3 meters. For 25× zoom: ~75m face ID range.

4_tandemvu_vs_standard.principle: TandemVu provides simultaneous panoramic overview + PTZ detail in one unit

standard_ptz.view: Single view — either wide OR zoomed, not both
standard_ptz.blind_spot: When zoomed in, rest of scene is unmonitored
standard_ptz.tracking: Loses overview when tracking a target

tandemvu.view: Dual view — panoramic overview always visible + independent PTZ zoom
tandemvu.blind_spot: None — panoramic channel always covers full scene
tandemvu.tracking: PTZ tracks target while panoramic maintains overview

4_tandemvu_vs_standard.recommendation: For critical areas where losing overview during zoom is unacceptable → TandemVu. For budget or simple tracking → Standard PTZ.

5_auto_tracking.principle: Auto-tracking automatically follows a moving target across the PTZ field of view

types.description: AI-based human/vehicle tracking, maintains lock on classified target
Follows any moving object, may lose target in busy scenes

5_auto_tracking.note: Auto-tracking requires clear scene. Dense crowds or many simultaneous targets will reduce effectiveness.

6_power_and_cabling.principle: PTZ cameras require higher power than fixed cameras

power_options.note: Requires Hi-PoE injector or compatible switch

power_options.suitable_for: All large PTZ, especially with heater
power_options.note: Most reliable for cold climate with heater

6_power_and_cabling.heater_note: PTZ cameras in cold climates (<-20°C) require heater. Budget additional 5-15W for heater/demister. 24VAC or Hi-PoE recommended for heated models.

7_mounting_and_brackets.principle: PTZ mounting must support weight, wind load, and provide clear field of view

mounting_options.best_for: Indoor ceilings, covered walkways, parking garages
Building walls, poles with wall adapter

height_recommendation.standard: 4-6m for parking lots and building perimeter
height_recommendation.note: Higher mounting = wider overview but less facial detail. Balance height vs zoom capability.

7_mounting_and_brackets.wind_load: For exposed pole mounts, calculate wind load. PTZ dome acts as sail. Use pole rated for local wind speed + camera weight.

8_preset_tour_pattern.principle: PTZ presets, tours, and patterns automate surveillance coverage

definitions.preset: Saved position (pan/tilt/zoom coordinates). Call by number to instantly move camera to that view. Typical: 300 presets.
definitions.patrol_tour: Sequence of presets with configurable dwell time at each. Camera moves through presets automatically. Typical: 8 tours.
definitions.pattern: Recorded manual movement path. Camera replays the exact path continuously. Typical: 4 patterns.
definitions.auto_scan: Camera slowly pans between two preset points continuously.

8_preset_tour_pattern.design_guide: Define presets for all critical views (gates, doors, parking rows). Create patrol tour covering all presets with 10-15s dwell each. Set as default action when no operator control.

9_recorder_compatibility.principle: NVR/DVR must support PTZ control protocol and resolution

ip_ptz.nvr: Any Hikvision NVR (via ONVIF or Hikvision protocol)
ip_ptz.control: Network PTZ control — no additional wiring
ip_ptz.acusense_note: AcuSense PTZ features require NXI AcuSense NVR for full AI integration

analog_ptz.control: Hikvision-C coax telemetry (control over same coax cable) — NO RS-485 needed

10_compliance.principle: PTZ cameras must meet safety, EMC, weather, and cybersecurity standards

standards.scope: Safety of PTZ electronics and motor
US equivalent of IP66 for outdoor enclosures
TVS 6000V for network interface. Surge protector recommended for exposed pole installations.
